rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment rollout is stuck because the new container image tag 'quay.io/kubernaut-cicd/demo-http-server:99.99.99-doesnotexist' does not exist in the registry, causing all new pods in ReplicaSet 'checkout-api-659cf7fcb6' to fail with ImagePullBackOff. The 120-second progressDeadlineSeconds was exceeded, triggering a ProgressDeadlineExceeded condition. The rolling update strategy partially scaled down the old RS (3→2 healthy pods), reducing availability. Rolling back to the previous revision restores the working 'demo-http-server:1.0.0' image.
contributingFactors: Invalid/non-existent image tag '99.99.99-doesnotexist' pushed to the deployment via 'kubectl set image'
New ReplicaSet 'checkout-api-659cf7fcb6' unable to start any pods — all stuck in ImagePullBackOff
Deployment progressDeadlineSeconds=120 exceeded, triggering ProgressDeadlineExceeded condition
Rolling update strategy (maxSurge:1, maxUnavailable:1) scaled down old RS from 3→2, reducing availability during the stuck rollout
No prior remediation history — this is the first rollout failure for this deployment

kaResponse.analysis: Deployment rollout is stuck because the new container image tag 'quay.io/kubernaut-cicd/demo-http-server:99.99.99-doesnotexist' does not exist in the registry, causing all new pods in ReplicaSet 'checkout-api-659cf7fcb6' to fail with ImagePullBackOff. The 120-second progressDeadlineSeconds was exceeded, triggering a ProgressDeadlineExceeded condition. The rolling update strategy partially scaled down the old RS (3→2 healthy pods), reducing availability. Rolling back to the previous revision restores the working 'demo-http-server:1.0.0' image.
